name: Rosalind Franklin rover
fact: ESA's first Mars rover. Originally a Russian-led joint mission planned for 2022; cancelled after the 2022 invasion of Ukraine and rescoped as ESA-led with NASA contributions. Re-targeted for 2028 launch, 2029 arrival. Carries a 2-metre subsurface drill — the deepest yet planned for a Mars rover, designed to reach below the radiation-damaged top metre.
capability: Will reach 2 m subsurface depth; the first directly-targeted astrobiology mission since Viking.
